# Data Integrity Checklist: LSP Client Integration

**Purpose**: Validate SPEC-008 data-integrity requirements for LSP edge verification, correction, suppression, provenance, audit metadata, and graph stability.
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are unique-target requirements defined before any LSP result may replace or suppress an existing graph edge? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-029-FR-030, Contract §Normalization]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec define an active-edge uniqueness rule so a corrected LSP edge cannot coexist with the old static or heuristic edge for the same semantic reference? [Completeness, Spec §FR-039, Data Model §EdgeVerificationWorkItem, Contract §Inputs]
- [x] CHK003 Is the replace-versus-suppress requirement specific enough to ensure one surviving active edge for in-workspace corrections and no new active edge for external, generated, or unindexed targets? [Completeness, Spec §FR-040, Contract §Result Rules]
- [ ] CHK004 Is correction metadata required to preserve previous provenance, previous target, LSP target, correction reason, language, server, and timestamp? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018a, Data Model §CorrectionMetadata, Contract §Correction Metadata]
- [ ] CHK005 Are `provenance: "lsp"` semantics limited to active edges that LSP verified or corrected, while untouched static/null and heuristic edges retain existing semantics? [Completeness, Spec §FR-016-FR-017, Data Model §EdgeVerificationResult]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is LSP target uniqueness defined after normalizing both `Location` and `LocationLink` responses and deduplicating equivalent ranges? [Clarity, Spec §FR-029, Data Model §NormalizedLspTarget]
- [ ] CHK007 Are in-workspace corrections required to resolve to exactly one compatible CodeGraph node before graph structure changes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-030, Data Model §NormalizedLspTarget]
- [x] CHK008 Are suppressed audit-only edges explicitly excluded from traversal, callers, callees, impact, and search/flow surfaces? [Clarity, Spec §FR-041, Data Model §EdgeVerificationResult, Contract §Correction Metadata]
- [ ] CHK009 Are result categories mutually exclusive enough for status counters to avoid double-counting checked, verified, corrected, suppressed, skipped, ambiguous, and degraded edges? [Clarity, Spec §FR-031, Data Model §EdgeVerificationResult, Contract §Result Rules]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK010 Are ambiguous and multi-definition LSP responses specified to leave existing graph edges unchanged and create no speculative replacement edges? [Coverage, Spec §FR-019, Spec §SC-007, Contract §Result Rules]
- [ ] CHK011 Are generated, external-library, and unindexed unique targets covered without creating external graph nodes? [Coverage, Spec §FR-030, Data Model §NormalizedLspTarget, Contract §Result Rules]
- [ ] CHK012 Are no-target, malformed-response, missing-server, crashed-server, and timeout scenarios specified to preserve existing graph structure? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-038, Contract §Result Rules]
- [ ] CHK013 Are existing `null`, `heuristic`, and prior `lsp` provenance cases covered for correction, suppression, and unchanged verification outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §FR-016-FR-018a, Data Model §EdgeVerificationWorkItem]
- [ ] CHK014 Are incremental watch corrections bounded so skipped or degraded watch batches cannot partially mutate graph edges outside the bounded work set? [Coverage, Spec §FR-035-FR-035a, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Are success criteria measurable for preventing noisy duplicate active edges and preserving node/edge count stability aside from intentional correction or suppression effects? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-010, Plan §Validation Strategy, Contract §Invariants]
- [ ] CHK016 Are correction fixtures required to prove old wrong targets are replaced or suppressed with audit metadata rather than left active beside LSP-corrected edges? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-006, Plan §Validation Strategy]
- [ ] CHK017 Are ambiguity fixtures required to prove zero speculative replacement edges from multi-target or multi-node LSP output? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-007, Plan §Validation Strategy]
- [ ] CHK018 Are callers, callees, impact, and search regression probes specified for LSP-enabled and non-LSP paths where corrected or suppressed edges may affect traversal? [Acceptance Criteria, Plan §Validation Strategy, Plan §Vertical PR Slices]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK019 Are data-integrity requirements aligned with the project constitution's deterministic, no-speculative-edges rule? [Consistency, Constitution §V, Spec §FR-019]
- [ ] CHK020 Are spec artifacts free of outbound links and external baseline project names while still preserving the internal parity baseline and public LSP terminology required by the feature? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Prerequisite and Version Evidence Policy]
- [ ] CHK021 Are audit requirements queryable enough for status/debug output without making suppressed edges active graph structure? [Dependency, Data Model §CorrectionMetadata, Contract §Correction Metadata]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: unique-target correction rules, provenance semantics, correction metadata, ambiguous/generated/external/unindexed LSP output, duplicate-edge prevention, traversal exclusion for suppressed audit data, and node/edge count stability.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer checklist for Phase 4 data-integrity readiness.
- Actor/timing: PR reviewer during SPEC-008 Phase 4 checklist gate.
- Explicit must-have items incorporated: avoid noisy duplicate edges that degrade callers/impact; suppressed audit-only edges must not remain active in traversal; ambiguous LSP output must not create speculative edges; no external baseline project names or outbound links in spec artifacts.
- Remediation rerun: CHK002, CHK003, CHK008, and CHK015 were re-evaluated after spec/plan/data-model/contract updates on 2026-07-05; no data-integrity gaps remain.
